findings.justification: The AJAX handler registered on wp_ajax_um_dismiss_notice only calls UM()->admin()->check_ajax_nonce() (nonce verification only) with no current_user_can() check, allowing any logged-in user who holds the um-admin-nonce to suppress arbitrary admin notices.

The admin_init handler processes $_REQUEST['um_dismiss_notice'] and verifies a nonce via wp_verify_nonce() but performs no capability check, so any logged-in user possessing the um-admin-nonce can permanently dismiss admin notices via a GET/POST request.

The _e() function is used to output a string containing raw HTML markup; if a translation file were compromised, arbitrary HTML could be injected into the admin page since _e() does not escape output the way esc_html_e() would.
